summary: Russian forces reported extensive UAV attacks across multiple fronts, with Ukrainian air defenses downing 92 drones and Russian strikes hitting logistics facilities in Zaporizhzhia. Russian claims of liberating Konstantinovka and surrounding areas include occupation of hundreds of buildings and significant Ukrainian losses. Ground combat continues in the east and south with both sides reporting casualties and equipment losses.
key_points: Russian drones struck a logistics building in Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight people and causing fire and vehicle damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 92 enemy drones, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Iltamass types, after 99 attack drones were launched (Source: @kpszsu/65649).
Russian military announced the liberation of Konstantinovka, occupying 627 buildings and claiming over 540 Ukrainian casualties and loss of 160 weapons (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on targets in Zaporizhzhia region and Donetsk (Source: @kpszsu/65622).

Multiple UAVs were reported over Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava, Kharkiv, Sumy, Chernihiv, and Odesa regions, heading toward various towns (Sources: @kpszsu/65648, @kpszsu/65641, @kpszsu/65637, @kpszsu/65619, @kpszsu/65620, @kpszsu/65621, @kpszsu/65631).
Seven Ukrainian UAVs were destroyed over Tula region during night operations (Source: @tass_agency/381565).
Air defense systems were active over Rostov, Crimea, Sevastopol, Melitopol, and Kirillovka, shooting down two UAVs near Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces intensified UAV attacks, with Ukrainian defenses downing 92 drones across the country. Russia claimed the liberation of Konstantinovka, reporting occupation of hundreds of buildings and heavy Ukrainian casualties. Ground fighting and air strikes continue on multiple fronts, affecting logistics hubs and civilian areas.

points: Ukrainian air defense units intercepted 92 enemy drones, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Iltamass types, after a wave of 99 attack drones was launched from directions including Orel, Bryansk, and Primorsko-Akhtarsk (Source: @kpszsu/65649).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a logistics operator building in Zaporizhzhia, resulting in eight injuries, a fire, and damage to several vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).

Ukrainian troops engaged in defensive battles in the eastern Dnipropetrovsk region, with strike drones heading toward Pavlograd and multiple UAV sightings near Zarychne and Samara settlements (Sources: @kpszsu/65637, @kpszsu/65648).
In the Chernihiv direction, strike drones were observed moving toward Borzna, indicating continued aerial pressure on northern fronts (Source: @kpszsu/65620).
Ukrainian air defenses were active over Rostov, Crimea, Sevastopol, Melitopol, and Kirillovka, shooting down two UAVs near Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, highlighting maritime aerial threats (Source: @kpszsu/65621).
Ukrainian units faced Russian advances in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict of Konstantinovka, with reports of surrounded units being eliminated (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).

Russian military announced the liberation of Konstantinovka, stating occupation of 627 buildings and elimination of surrounded Ukrainian units in the southwestern part of the city (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Russian forces reported seizing 37 strongholds and 251 buildings in Krasy Liman, with Ukrainian losses of 185 personnel and 45 weapons (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
The Russian Defense Ministry claimed capture of the strategic area Ray-Aleksandrovka in the Slavyansk direction, located 15 km from Slavyansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on targets in the Zaporizhzhia region and Donetsk, indicating escalation of aerial bombardment (Source: @kpszsu/65622).
Seven Ukrainian UAVs were destroyed over the Tula region during night operations, reflecting Russian air defense effectiveness (Source: @tass_agency/381565).
Russian reports indicated ongoing UAV attacks from multiple directions, including Orel, Bryansk, and Primorsko-Akhtarsk, targeting northern, southern, and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65649).
Russian forces continued offensive operations in the eastern Zaporozhzhia region, with GRU "East" units advancing toward Lyubitskoe and Zarnitsa settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
UAV groups were observed east of Kharkiv near Savintsi moving westward, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions in the eastern Kharkiv sector (Source: @kpszsu/65619).
Strike drones were reported in the east of Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Pavlograd, indicating pressure on industrial areas (Source: @kpszsu/65653).
UAV activity was noted on the border of Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, moving northward, potentially targeting logistics corridors (Source: @kpszsu/65641).
Russian GRU "East" units continued penetrating defenses toward Lyubitskoe and Zarnitsa in the eastern Zaporozhzhia region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
UAVs were reported heading toward Komyshuvakha in Zaporizhzhia, adding to aerial threats in the eastern theater (Source: @kpszsu/65631).
Russian forces claimed advances in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict of Konstantinovka, engaging Ukrainian units in close combat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
UAV sightings over the Donetsk region moving toward Kharkiv indicate cross-front aerial movements (Source: @kpszsu/65642).
UAVs were reported over Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Pavlograd, with some originating from the north, suggesting northern approach vectors (Source: @kpszsu/65648).
UAVs were observed north of the Samara settlement in Dnipropetrovsk moving westward, indicating activity from northern directions (Source: @kpszsu/65641).
UAVs on the border of Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk were heading north, potentially targeting supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/65641).
Air defense systems over Rostov, Crimea, Sevastopol, Melitopol, and Kirillovka engaged aerial threats, reflecting heightened northern and southern air activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Seven Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Tula region, demonstrating Russian air defense success in the north-central sector (Source: @tass_agency/381565).
UAVs were reported in Poltava heading toward Kremenchuk, a central-northern corridor (Source: @kpszsu/65642).
UAVs were observed in the Lozova district of Kharkiv heading north‑west, indicating northern maneuvering in the Kharkiv area (Source: @kpszsu/65642).
Russian UAV strike on a logistics building in Zaporizhzhia caused eight injuries, fire, and vehicle damage, highlighting southern operational impact (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Guided aviation bombs were launched by Russian tactical aviation on targets in the Zaporizhzhia region and Donetsk, intensifying southern bombardment (Source: @kpszsu/65622).
UAVs were reported from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, indicating maritime southern aerial threats (Source: @kpszsu/65621).
UAV activity was noted over Zaporizhzhia near Vylnyansk moving northward, suggesting southern-to-northern flight paths (Source: @kpszsu/65648).
UAVs were observed heading toward Komyshuvakha in Zaporizhzhia, adding to southern front pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65631).
Russian forces reported strikes on Kharkov, Sumy, and Zaporozhye, affecting southern operational zones (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
UAVs were observed over Donetsk moving toward Kharkiv, indicating cross‑regional southern operations (Source: @kpszsu/65642).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 92 enemy drones, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Iltamass types, after a wave of 99 attack drones (Source: @kpszsu/65649).
Two UAVs were shot down near Sevastopol as air defense systems operated over Crimea and the Black Sea region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Seven Ukrainian UAVs were destroyed over the Tula region during night operations, reflecting Russian air defense capability (Source: @tass_agency/381565).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, indicating increased use of precision air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/65622).

Multiple UAV sightings across Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava, Kharkiv, Sumy, Chernihiv, and Odesa illustrate widespread aerial activity on both sides (Sources: @kpszsu/65648, @kpszsu/65641, @kpszsu/65621, @kpszsu/65631).
Air defense systems were active over Rostov, Crimea, Sevastopol, Melitopol, and Kirillovka, engaging numerous aerial threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Russian forces reported UAV attacks from Orel, Bryansk, and Primorsko‑Akhtarsk directions, showing diversified launch points (Source: @kpszsu/65649).

key_developments: Russian forces launched 99 attack drones of Shahed, Gerbera, Iltamass and imitation types from Orel, Bryansk and Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, with Ukrainian defenses downing 92 of them (Source: @kpszsu/65649).
A Russian UAV strike hit a logistics building in Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight, causing fire and vehicle damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Russia announced the liberation of Konstantinovka, occupying 627 buildings and claiming over 540 Ukrainian casualties and loss of 160 weapons (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down two UAVs near Sevastopol while maintaining coverage over Rostov, Crimea, Melitopol and Kirillovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31016).
Guided bombs were deployed by Russian tactical aviation on targets in Zaporizhzhia region and Donetsk, escalating aerial bombardment (Source: @kpszsu/65622).
UAVs were observed from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, indicating maritime aerial threats to southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/65621).
Seven Ukrainian UAVs were destroyed over Tula region during night operations, showing Russian air defense effectiveness (Source: @tass_agency/381565).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian air defenses are proving effective against large-scale UAV attacks, yet Russian drone strikes continue to target logistical hubs and civilian infrastructure, notably in Zaporizhzhia. Russian ground advances in Konstantinovka and Krasy Liman suggest a focus on consolidating control over occupied territories, increasing pressure on Ukrainian supply lines. The proliferation of UAV activity across multiple fronts indicates a sustained aerial threat that could complicate Ukrainian operational planning.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces are reallocating air defense assets to protect critical southern and eastern corridors, while also reinforcing ground positions in contested towns such as Konstantinovka. Russian command appears to be leveraging both UAV swarms and guided bomb strikes to degrade Ukrainian logistics and morale. International observers note the escalation of drone warfare as a key factor shaping the conflict's dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV launch sites in Orel, Bryansk and Primorsko‑Akhtarsk is essential to anticipate future attack waves. The reported losses of Ukrainian personnel and equipment in Konstantinovka require verification through open‑source imagery. The emergence of maritime‑origin UAVs toward Odesa suggests potential new launch platforms that warrant close surveillance.
